revision.comment: Bot: migrate talk page structure; import discussion seed (en:Amago clan); add local discussion section + dated import note
revision.text:
{{talk page header}}
<!-- This talk page covers the main article and all associated namespace layers --> <!-- Imported from Japanese/English Wikipedia talk pages on 2026-02-25 (UTC) when available. -->
== Local discussion == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:19, 25 February 2026 (UTC) <!-- Add local discussion below this line. -->
== Imported from English Wikipedia (2026-02-25, [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/1198525843 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:19, 25 February 2026 (UTC) <!-- Source: en:Talk:Amago clan | revid=1198525843 --> {{WikiProject banner shell|class=Start| {{WikiProject Military history|class=Start <!-- 1. It is suitably referenced, and all major points have appropriate inline citations. --> |B-Class-1=no <!-- 2. It reasonably covers the topic, and does not contain major omissions or inaccuracies. --> |B-Class-2=no <!-- 3. It has a defined structure, including a lead section and one or more sections of content. --> |B-Class-3=no <!-- 4. It is free from major grammatical errors. --> |B-Class-4=yes <!-- 5. It contains appropriate supporting materials, such as an infobox, images, or diagrams. --> |B-Class-5=no |Japanese-task-force=yes}} {{WikiProject Japan|importance=mid|milhist=y|history=y}} }}
==Vassals list== I don't think such a list belongs on the main page - it's just a lengthy list of names and ultimately adds little meaning or value. But I don't want to trash it entirely, so it's going here.
These samurai were vassals of Amago clan and listed by their date of birth. * [[Tako Tokitaka]] ([[1494]]-[[1562]]) * [[Akana Hisakiyo]] ([[1528]]-15??) * [[Tachihara Hisatsuna]] ([[1531]]-[[1613]]) * [[Misawa Tamekiyo]] ([[1537]]-[[1588]]) * [[Kamei Korenori]] ([[1557]]-[[1612]]) * [[Honjo Tsunemitsu]] (d. [[1562]]) * [[Uyama Hisakane]] (d. [[1566]]) * [[Oki Tamekiyo]] (d. [[1569]]) * [[Jinzai Motomichi]] (d. [[1578]]) * [[Mitoya Hisasuke]] (d. [[1591]]) * [[Kamei Hidetsuna]] * [[Kawazoe Hisamori]] * [[Maki Tomochika]] * [[Yonehara Tsunehiro]] (d. [[1613]]) * [[Daizai Takayoshi]] * [[Kumano Hisatada]] * [[Matsuda Masayasu]] * [[Sase Kiyomune]] * [[Ushio Yukikiyo]] * Ago Katsuhisa * Hirano Hisatoshi * Honda Huzennokami * Kumagaya Sinuemon * Moriwaki Ichimasa * Nakai Hisaie * Ogasawara Nagao * Yuhara Munetsune
:Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:19, 25 February 2026 (UTC) ==Amago or Amako?== The romanised spelling seems to be a contested issue, similar to the Asai/Azai controversy. The Japanese kanji reading itself implies that it can be pronounced either way (it's read Amako, but adding dakuten marks in the middle of words like this is fairly common, so it could be Amago). I've heard both used before, e.g. Amago on this article as it is now (3/3/14) and Amako in some sources e.g. the works of Stephen Turnbull. Is there a decisive answer to this?[[User:Homusubi244|Homusubi244]] ([[User talk:Homusubi244|talk]]) 18:37, 3 March 2014 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:19, 25 February 2026 (UTC)

revision.comment: Bot: migrate talk page structure; import discussion seed (en:Amago clan); add local discussion section + dated import note
revision.text:
{{talk page header}}

It is suitably referenced, and all major points have appropriate inline citations. --> |B-Class-1=no <!-- 2. It reasonably covers the topic, and does not contain major omissions or inaccuracies. --> |B-Class-2=no <!-- 3. It has a defined structure, including a lead section and one or more sections of content. --> |B-Class-3=no <!-- 4. It is free from major grammatical errors. --> |B-Class-4=yes <!-- 5. It contains appropriate supporting materials, such as an infobox, images, or diagrams. --> |B-Class-5=no |Japanese-task-force=yes}} {{WikiProject Japan|importance=mid|milhist=y|history=y}} }}
==Vassals list== I don't think such a list belongs on the main page - it's just a lengthy list of names and ultimately adds little meaning or value. But I don't want to trash it entirely, so it's going here.
These samurai were vassals of Amago clan and listed by their date of birth. * [[Tako Tokitaka]] ([[1494]]-[[1562]]) * [[Akana Hisakiyo]] ([[1528]]-15??) * [[Tachihara Hisatsuna]] ([[1531]]-[[1613]]) * [[Misawa Tamekiyo]] ([[1537]]-[[1588]]) * [[Kamei Korenori]] ([[1557]]-[[1612]]) * [[Honjo Tsunemitsu]] (d. [[1562]]) * [[Uyama Hisakane]] (d. [[1566]]) * [[Oki Tamekiyo]] (d. [[1569]]) * [[Jinzai Motomichi]] (d. [[1578]]) * [[Mitoya Hisasuke]] (d. [[1591]]) * [[Kamei Hidetsuna]] * [[Kawazoe Hisamori]] * [[Maki Tomochika]] * [[Yonehara Tsunehiro]] (d. [[1613]]) * [[Daizai Takayoshi]] * [[Kumano Hisatada]] * [[Matsuda Masayasu]] * [[Sase Kiyomune]] * [[Ushio Yukikiyo]] * Ago Katsuhisa * Hirano Hisatoshi * Honda Huzennokami * Kumagaya Sinuemon * Moriwaki Ichimasa * Nakai Hisaie * Ogasawara Nagao * Yuhara Munetsune
:Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:49, 25 February 2026 (UTC) ==Amago or Amako?== The romanised spelling seems to be a contested issue, similar to the Asai/Azai controversy. The Japanese kanji reading itself implies that it can be pronounced either way (it's read Amako, but adding dakuten marks in the middle of words like this is fairly common, so it could be Amago). I've heard both used before, e.g. Amago on this article as it is now (3/3/14) and Amako in some sources e.g. the works of Stephen Turnbull. Is there a decisive answer to this?[[User:Homusubi244|Homusubi244]] ([[User talk:Homusubi244|talk]]) 18:37, 3 March 2014 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:49, 25 February 2026 (UTC)

==Vassals list== I don't think such a list belongs on the main page - it's just a lengthy list of names and ultimately adds little meaning or value. But I don't want to trash it entirely, so it's going here.
These samurai were vassals of Amago clan and listed by their date of birth. * [[Tako Tokitaka]] ([[1494]]-[[1562]]) * [[Akana Hisakiyo]] ([[1528]]-15??) * [[Tachihara Hisatsuna]] ([[1531]]-[[1613]]) * [[Misawa Tamekiyo]] ([[1537]]-[[1588]]) * [[Kamei Korenori]] ([[1557]]-[[1612]]) * [[Honjo Tsunemitsu]] (d. [[1562]]) * [[Uyama Hisakane]] (d. [[1566]]) * [[Oki Tamekiyo]] (d. [[1569]]) * [[Jinzai Motomichi]] (d. [[1578]]) * [[Mitoya Hisasuke]] (d. [[1591]]) * [[Kamei Hidetsuna]] * [[Kawazoe Hisamori]] * [[Maki Tomochika]] * [[Yonehara Tsunehiro]] (d. [[1613]]) * [[Daizai Takayoshi]] * [[Kumano Hisatada]] * [[Matsuda Masayasu]] * [[Sase Kiyomune]] * [[Ushio Yukikiyo]] * Ago Katsuhisa * Hirano Hisatoshi * Honda Huzennokami * Kumagaya Sinuemon * Moriwaki Ichimasa * Nakai Hisaie * Ogasawara Nagao * Yuhara Munetsune
:Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:20, 25 February 2026 (UTC) ==Amago or Amako?== The romanised spelling seems to be a contested issue, similar to the Asai/Azai controversy. The Japanese kanji reading itself implies that it can be pronounced either way (it's read Amako, but adding dakuten marks in the middle of words like this is fairly common, so it could be Amago). I've heard both used before, e.g. Amago on this article as it is now (3/3/14) and Amako in some sources e.g. the works of Stephen Turnbull. Is there a decisive answer to this?[[User:Homusubi244|Homusubi244]] ([[User talk:Homusubi244|talk]]) 18:37, 3 March 2014 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:20, 25 February 2026 (UTC)

revision.comment: Bot: migrate talk page structure; import discussion seed (en:Amago clan); add local discussion section + dated import note
revision.text:
{{talk page header}}
<!-- This talk page covers the main article and all associated namespace layers --> <!-- Imported from Japanese/English/Simple English Wikipedia talk pages on 2026-02-25 (UTC) when available. -->
== Local discussion == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:35, 25 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:20, 25 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:49, 25 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:19, 25 February 2026 (UTC) <!-- Add local discussion below this line. -->
== Imported from English Wikipedia (2026-02-25, [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/1198525843 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:35, 25 February 2026 (UTC) <!-- Source: en:Talk:Amago clan | revid=1198525843 --> {{WikiProject banner shell|class=Start| {{WikiProject Military history|class=Start <!-- 1. It is suitably referenced, and all major points have appropriate inline citations. --> |B-Class-1=no <!-- 2. It reasonably covers the topic, and does not contain major omissions or inaccuracies. --> |B-Class-2=no <!-- 3. It has a defined structure, including a lead section and one or more sections of content. --> |B-Class-3=no <!-- 4. It is free from major grammatical errors. --> |B-Class-4=yes <!-- 5. It contains appropriate supporting materials, such as an infobox, images, or diagrams. --> |B-Class-5=no |Japanese-task-force=yes}} {{WikiProject Japan|importance=mid|milhist=y|history=y}} }}
==Vassals list== I don't think such a list belongs on the main page - it's just a lengthy list of names and ultimately adds little meaning or value. But I don't want to trash it entirely, so it's going here.
These samurai were vassals of Amago clan and listed by their date of birth. * [[Tako Tokitaka]] ([[1494]]-[[1562]]) * [[Akana Hisakiyo]] ([[1528]]-15??) * [[Tachihara Hisatsuna]] ([[1531]]-[[1613]]) * [[Misawa Tamekiyo]] ([[1537]]-[[1588]]) * [[Kamei Korenori]] ([[1557]]-[[1612]]) * [[Honjo Tsunemitsu]] (d. [[1562]]) * [[Uyama Hisakane]] (d. [[1566]]) * [[Oki Tamekiyo]] (d. [[1569]]) * [[Jinzai Motomichi]] (d. [[1578]]) * [[Mitoya Hisasuke]] (d. [[1591]]) * [[Kamei Hidetsuna]] * [[Kawazoe Hisamori]] * [[Maki Tomochika]] * [[Yonehara Tsunehiro]] (d. [[1613]]) * [[Daizai Takayoshi]] * [[Kumano Hisatada]] * [[Matsuda Masayasu]] * [[Sase Kiyomune]] * [[Ushio Yukikiyo]] * Ago Katsuhisa * Hirano Hisatoshi * Honda Huzennokami * Kumagaya Sinuemon * Moriwaki Ichimasa * Nakai Hisaie * Ogasawara Nagao * Yuhara Munetsune
:Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:35, 25 February 2026 (UTC) ==Amago or Amako?== The romanised spelling seems to be a contested issue, similar to the Asai/Azai controversy. The Japanese kanji reading itself implies that it can be pronounced either way (it's read Amako, but adding dakuten marks in the middle of words like this is fairly common, so it could be Amago). I've heard both used before, e.g. Amago on this article as it is now (3/3/14) and Amako in some sources e.g. the works of Stephen Turnbull. Is there a decisive answer to this?[[User:Homusubi244|Homusubi244]] ([[User talk:Homusubi244|talk]]) 18:37, 3 March 2014 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:35, 25 February 2026 (UTC)

revision.comment: Bot: migrate talk page structure; import discussion seed (en:Amago clan); add local discussion section + dated import note
revision.text:
{{talk page header}}
<!-- This talk page covers the main article and all associated namespace layers --> <!-- Imported from Japanese/English/Simple English Wikipedia talk pages on 2026-02-26 (UTC) when available. -->
== Local discussion == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:38, 26 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:35, 25 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:20, 25 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:49, 25 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:19, 25 February 2026 (UTC) <!-- Add local discussion below this line. -->
== Imported from English Wikipedia (2026-02-26, [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/1198525843 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:38, 26 February 2026 (UTC) <!-- Source: en:Talk:Amago clan | revid=1198525843 --> {{WikiProject banner shell|class=Start| {{WikiProject Military history|class=Start <!-- 1. It is suitably referenced, and all major points have appropriate inline citations. --> |B-Class-1=no <!-- 2. It reasonably covers the topic, and does not contain major omissions or inaccuracies. --> |B-Class-2=no <!-- 3. It has a defined structure, including a lead section and one or more sections of content. --> |B-Class-3=no <!-- 4. It is free from major grammatical errors. --> |B-Class-4=yes <!-- 5. It contains appropriate supporting materials, such as an infobox, images, or diagrams. --> |B-Class-5=no |Japanese-task-force=yes}} {{WikiProject Japan|importance=mid|milhist=y|history=y}} }}
==Vassals list== I don't think such a list belongs on the main page - it's just a lengthy list of names and ultimately adds little meaning or value. But I don't want to trash it entirely, so it's going here.
These samurai were vassals of Amago clan and listed by their date of birth. * [[Tako Tokitaka]] ([[1494]]-[[1562]]) * [[Akana Hisakiyo]] ([[1528]]-15??) * [[Tachihara Hisatsuna]] ([[1531]]-[[1613]]) * [[Misawa Tamekiyo]] ([[1537]]-[[1588]]) * [[Kamei Korenori]] ([[1557]]-[[1612]]) * [[Honjo Tsunemitsu]] (d. [[1562]]) * [[Uyama Hisakane]] (d. [[1566]]) * [[Oki Tamekiyo]] (d. [[1569]]) * [[Jinzai Motomichi]] (d. [[1578]]) * [[Mitoya Hisasuke]] (d. [[1591]]) * [[Kamei Hidetsuna]] * [[Kawazoe Hisamori]] * [[Maki Tomochika]] * [[Yonehara Tsunehiro]] (d. [[1613]]) * [[Daizai Takayoshi]] * [[Kumano Hisatada]] * [[Matsuda Masayasu]] * [[Sase Kiyomune]] * [[Ushio Yukikiyo]] * Ago Katsuhisa * Hirano Hisatoshi * Honda Huzennokami * Kumagaya Sinuemon * Moriwaki Ichimasa * Nakai Hisaie * Ogasawara Nagao * Yuhara Munetsune
:Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:38, 26 February 2026 (UTC) ==Amago or Amako?== The romanised spelling seems to be a contested issue, similar to the Asai/Azai controversy. The Japanese kanji reading itself implies that it can be pronounced either way (it's read Amako, but adding dakuten marks in the middle of words like this is fairly common, so it could be Amago). I've heard both used before, e.g. Amago on this article as it is now (3/3/14) and Amako in some sources e.g. the works of Stephen Turnbull. Is there a decisive answer to this?[[User:Homusubi244|Homusubi244]] ([[User talk:Homusubi244|talk]]) 18:37, 3 March 2014 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:38, 26 February 2026 (UTC)

revision.comment: [[Project:History cleanup|Offloading history due to miraheze stability concerns]] previous contributors were [[User:en>Citation bot]], [[User:Immanuelle]], see [https://emmaleonhart.github.io/shintowiki-scripts/wikihistory.html?page=Amago_clan here] for full history [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24756154998|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!-- History offloaded: due to miraheze stability concerns, the edit history of this page has been offloaded to the XML archive. See https://emmaleonhart.github.io/shintowiki-scripts/wikihistory.html?page=Amago_clan for full history. Last refreshed: [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24756154998|commit triggered pipeline]] --> {{talk page header}}
<!-- This talk page covers the main article and all associated namespace layers --> <!-- Imported from Japanese/English/Simple English Wikipedia talk pages on 2026-02-26 (UTC) when available. -->
== Local discussion == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:38, 26 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:35, 25 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:20, 25 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:49, 25 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:19, 25 February 2026 (UTC) <!-- Add local discussion below this line. -->

It has a defined structure, including a lead section and one or more sections of content. --> |B-Class-3=no <!-- 4. It is free from major grammatical errors. --> |B-Class-4=yes <!-- 5. It contains appropriate supporting materials, such as an infobox, images, or diagrams. --> |B-Class-5=no |Japanese-task-force=yes}} {{WikiProject Japan|importance=mid|milhist=y|history=y}} }}
==Vassals list== I don't think such a list belongs on the main page - it's just a lengthy list of names and ultimately adds little meaning or value. But I don't want to trash it entirely, so it's going here.
These samurai were vassals of Amago clan and listed by their date of birth. * [[Tako Tokitaka]] ([[1494]]-[[1562]]) * [[Akana Hisakiyo]] ([[1528]]-15??) * [[Tachihara Hisatsuna]] ([[1531]]-[[1613]]) * [[Misawa Tamekiyo]] ([[1537]]-[[1588]]) * [[Kamei Korenori]] ([[1557]]-[[1612]]) * [[Honjo Tsunemitsu]] (d. [[1562]]) * [[Uyama Hisakane]] (d. [[1566]]) * [[Oki Tamekiyo]] (d. [[1569]]) * [[Jinzai Motomichi]] (d. [[1578]]) * [[Mitoya Hisasuke]] (d. [[1591]]) * [[Kamei Hidetsuna]] * [[Kawazoe Hisamori]] * [[Maki Tomochika]] * [[Yonehara Tsunehiro]] (d. [[1613]]) * [[Daizai Takayoshi]] * [[Kumano Hisatada]] * [[Matsuda Masayasu]] * [[Sase Kiyomune]] * [[Ushio Yukikiyo]] * Ago Katsuhisa * Hirano Hisatoshi * Honda Huzennokami * Kumagaya Sinuemon * Moriwaki Ichimasa * Nakai Hisaie * Ogasawara Nagao * Yuhara Munetsune
:Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:38, 26 February 2026 (UTC) ==Amago or Amako?== The romanised spelling seems to be a contested issue, similar to the Asai/Azai controversy. The Japanese kanji reading itself implies that it can be pronounced either way (it's read Amako, but adding dakuten marks in the middle of words like this is fairly common, so it could be Amago). I've heard both used before, e.g. Amago on this article as it is now (3/3/14) and Amako in some sources e.g. the works of Stephen Turnbull. Is there a decisive answer to this?[[User:Homusubi244|Homusubi244]] ([[User talk:Homusubi244|talk]]) 18:37, 3 March 2014 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:38, 26 February 2026 (UTC)
